summary: Adversarial gap-spotting over the AE101 theory handbook corpus
logs: verify: 4 confirmed / 11 downgraded / 6 refuted / 10 minors unverified

finding.title: The far half of the map never closes: no M5/M6 beat consolidates Verification/Absorption/Outcome, breaking the corpus's own promises ('laws named at M5 and M6', 'every module points back to this map')
finding.evidence: Five lenses independently filed this as their top finding. In-room promises: the-whole-map.md (M2) 'Every module points back to this map. The question is always the same: which phase is this move in, and what does that phase govern?' and the-far-half.md:23-24 (M4) 'The laws that explain the bite get named at M5 and M6, against what your own run did.' The near half is paid in full — the-loop-half-filled.md (M3 close) delivers per-law NAME + MAP-POSITION + MECHANISM + GOVERNOR. The far half gets no twin: grep of every M5/M6 in-room lecture body above the fence (what-packaging-is, learning-through-contrast, quality-is-grounding, steering-the-wiring, the-2-frontiers, the-loop-has-a-name, story-of-module-6, composing-the-workflow, agents-that-build-agents) for map/Absorption/Outcome/phase vocabulary: zero Field-Map references after the M4 opener. The laws that do arrive in-room (three-pattern, eval) are never placed on a phase — only the dual-wired reading-the-return self-locates ('On the map, they live in the Work phase'). The map's ghost strand 'THE CHECKING LOOP — by hand for now' is drawn at M2 and never un-ghosted when the student builds exactly that at M5-M6. The student is told 'Material with a location is material you can find again later', then half the training's material gets no location — a durability reader ends with a six-phase map annotated on three phases, and the 'too little theory' failure the layer was built to fix re-arms for M4-M6.
finding.where_it_would_live: A far-half twin of curriculum/lectures/the-loop-half-filled.md in M6's closer chain (before or inside the-loop-has-a-name.md), naming per-law {NAME · MAP-POSITION · MECHANISM · GOVERNOR} for Verification/Absorption/Outcome; plus one map-position clause each in what-packaging-is.md (three-pattern → Verification) and the-loop-has-a-name.md (eval → the map's checking loop, built, un-ghosting the M2 strand).

coverage.reason: The gap is real and exactly as claimed. The in-room promises exist: the-whole-map.md line 114 (M2) says "Every module points back to this map" and the-far-half.md line 23 (M4) says "The laws that explain the bite get named at M5 and M6." The near half gets its consolidation twin — the-loop-half-filled.md (M3) names five laws each with an explicit map position ("(Work)", "(Context)", "(Intent)", "(spans the loop)") plus a governor. The far half gets none: a full read of all nine M5/M6 in-room lecture bodies above the fence (learning-through-contrast, what-packaging-is, quality-is-grounding, steering-the-wiring, the-2-frontiers, the-loop-has-a-name, story-of-module-6, composing-the-workflow, agents-that-build-agents) plus greps for map/phase/Verification/Absorption/Outcome/govern/checking-loop/far-half vocabulary found zero Field-Map references. The laws that do arrive in-room (three-pattern in what-packaging-is.md, eval in the-loop-has-a-name.md) are never placed on a phase, and the M2 ghost strand "◌ THE CHECKING LOOP ... by hand for now" appears only in the-whole-map.md and is never un-ghosted when the student builds exactly that at M5–M6. Decisively, the corpus's own maintainer contract in the-far-half.md line 38 states: "Per-law {NAME · MAP-POSITION · MECHANISM · GOVERNOR} for Verification/Absorption/Outcome laws is the M5/M6 closer job, not this opener's" — the job is explicitly assigned to the M5/M6 closers, and no M5/M6 closer performs it. This is a promised, contract-owed beat that no in-room file delivers.
coverage.counter_evidence: The only student-visible map references after the M4 opener, neither of which refutes: (1) reading-the-return.md line 20 (M4 in-room): "On the map, they live in the Work phase. The failure happens where the work happens. Reading for them is the far half's first verification move." — a single self-location of the three lenses in the WORK phase, already acknowledged by the finder, and it places nothing on Verification/Absorption/Outcome as named laws. (2) backpressure.md line 26 (M5 SUPPLEMENTARY, optional pre-read): "On the near half of the map, you were the feedback... On the far half, you engineer your replacement in that path and keep the judgment." — one sentence of map-position vocabulary in at-risk delivery, covering only the feedback-position change, not a per-law consolidation of the three far-half phases; Absorption and Outcome are never map-placed anywhere after the M4 opener, in-room or supplementary. Also verification-asymmetry.md line 13 (M4 supplementary) mentions the asymmetry "runs through the whole far half of the map" — again supplementary and again not a consolidation.

materiality.reason: The gap is material by the training's own causal theory of its failure, not by a completeness fetish. Verified facts: (1) grep of every M5/M6 in-room lecture body above the maintainer fence (what-packaging-is, learning-through-contrast, quality-is-grounding, steering-the-wiring, the-2-frontiers, the-loop-has-a-name, story-of-module-6, composing-the-workflow, agents-that-build-agents) confirms zero Field-Map/phase placements after the M4 opener — only reading-the-return (M4) self-locates. (2) The corpus explicitly owes this beat to itself: curriculum/lectures/the-far-half.md maintainer block — "Per-law {NAME · MAP-POSITION · MECHANISM · GOVERNOR} for Verification/Absorption/Outcome laws is the M5/M6 closer job"; that job is unpaid, while the near half got its twin (the-loop-half-filled.md, full per-law placement + a carried governor). (3) The training's own hard gate declares unplaced laws blocking: theory-evals.md B★ — "unplaced (no map phase)" = BLOCK, never averaged. (4) In-room promises to the student go unredeemed: "Every module points back to this map" (the-whole-map.md) and "The laws that explain the bite get named at M5 and M6" (the-far-half.md) — the laws arrive (three-pattern, eval primitive) but are never placed or consolidated, and the ghost "CHECKING LOOP — by hand for now" strand is never drawn solid even though M5–M6 is where the student builds exactly that. Materiality for the Monday practitioner: the reasoning tool the training installs ("which phase is this move in, and what does that phase govern?") is unusable for precisely the phases where the practitioner's new Monday questions live — do I trust this unattended run (Verification), how much output can I actually take up (Absorption), what survives the run (Outcome). theory-plan.md's own finding says "Verification is by far the densest phase... agentic engineering is mostly a verification discipline" — so the un-consolidated half is the course's claimed center of gravity. The root-cause diagnosis of the "too little theory" cohort failure was theory-present-as-content-but-no-spine; the far half currently reproduces exactly that state, so the failure the layer was built to fix re-arms for M4–M6. Not REFUTED-by-design: theory-plan.md itself names the payoff beat as a candidate ("far-half re-show of the engine with the ghost drawn solid... 'the map, filled in'; M6 close") — the beat is owed and unbuilt, not descoped.
materiality.counter_evidence: Honest weakeners, none decisive: (1) The far-half CONTENT largely exists in place — what-packaging-is.md gives the three-pattern per-failure-mode mechanisms plus the terrain/dead-reckoning picture (drift grows between checks; a check is a position fix; rails at irreversible edges), the-loop-has-a-name.md gives the eval primitive with mechanism and a scaling story, and supplementaries (backpressure.md, verification-asymmetry.md) carry far-half framing with two brief near/far-half references. A diligent reader is not left content-less; the missing layer is consolidation + placement, so the finding's "blocking" severity rests on accepting the training's own claim that the anchor layer, not the content, was the cohort failure. (2) The corpus is mid-flight: theory-plan v0.9 explicitly commissioned "completeness reviews of theory" and lists the M6-close map-filled-in beat as a candidate — this finding is the commissioned review finding the known-open debt, which confirms the gap is real but means severity reflects unfinished planned work rather than a design blind spot. (3) The finding's proposed fix is cheap relative to "blocking" language: one closer lecture plus two one-clause placements, suggesting the damage, while real, is concentrated and repairable rather than structural.

finding.title: Agent-directed security (lethal trifecta, instructions-vs-data inseparability) has zero guaranteed exposure while the in-room arc assembles all three trifecta legs — and 'prompt injection' appears nowhere in the corpus, supplementary included
finding.evidence: Five lenses converge. The corpus's only treatment of attacks-through-the-agent — 'An LLM cannot reliably tell instructions from data. Everything in the context window is one stream of tokens; no privileged channel marks which part came from you' — is supplementary/the-lethal-trifecta.md, wired as optional three times (plan-mode-done-right §Pre-reads, earn-the-trust prework, earn-the-trust §Next). Grep of all 22 in-room lecture bodies for injection/untrusted/trifecta: the only hits are a prompt key name and one mechanism-free clause in what-packaging-is.md:235 ('reading untrusted code'). In-room security (skills-from-the-frontier, M3) is STRIDE/least-privilege on the FEATURE; the trifecta page itself declares the gap: 'STRIDE models attacks on the system; the trifecta models attacks through the agent... A deployment needs both bounds.' Meanwhile guaranteed delivery escalates exactly the three legs: M1's first move outside the repo (connector + private repo), M4's multi-hour unattended run 'with your rules files, memory, ADRs, and skills loaded', M6's scheduled sweeps that read repos and open issues, and 19.2% auto-merge as the destination shape. Durability kicker: even the supplementary never uses the term 'prompt injection', so a student who DOES read it still lacks the one searchable handle the entire field uses (Willison, Anthropic guidance, every security post of 2025-26). The M2 map even projects a SECURITY LOOP strand that no in-room content ever fills for the agent itself — the first thing a hostile CISO checks.
finding.where_it_would_live: One in-room beat at the M4 send-off gate (curriculum/lectures/test-and-learn.md's 'One rule about running the experiment' slide or the-far-half.md — the trifecta page's own maintainer note names the leash-grant as the natural wiring) or at M3 close in skills-from-the-frontier.md; add the words 'prompt injection' to the trifecta body.

coverage.reason: Every factual leg of the finding survives adversarial search. (1) A synonym sweep (injection, trifecta, untrusted, malicious, adversar-, exfiltrat-, attack, sandbox, allowlist, credential, leak, Willison, OWASP, secur-, threat, STRIDE) across all 22 in-room lecture bodies above the maintainer fence yields exactly what the finder said: the prompt key `ae101-agent-loop-tool-injection` in the-agent-loop.md:24 — whose registry body (curriculum/prompts/ae101-agent-loop-tool-injection.md) is about MCP-connector tools landing in the tool set, not adversarial injection — and the mechanism-free clause "reading untrusted code" in what-packaging-is.md:235. skills-from-the-frontier.md is STRIDE/least-privilege on the FEATURE, as conceded. (2) All three trifecta wirings are explicitly labeled "Optional": earn-the-trust.md:14 ("Optional pre-reads in the Module 2 to Module 3 gap"), earn-the-trust.md:84 ("Optional forward-look... Before the agent gets a longer leash"), plan-mode-done-right.md:79 ("Optional. Lands the modern agentic-security frame"). (3) The exact string "prompt injection" appears in none of the 27 corpus files, supplementary body included — the supplementary teaches the mechanism without ever naming the field's searchable term. (4) The escalation claims check out in guaranteed delivery: run-the-first-experiment.md:73 sends the M4 run off "with your rules files, memory, ADRs, and skills loaded," and the 19.2% Intercom auto-merge shape is taught in-room twice (the-loop-has-a-name.md:16, what-packaging-is.md:247). (5) the-whole-map.md:56 does project a "THE SECURITY LOOP" strand no in-room content fills for the agent itself, and the supplementary's own maintainer note (line 36) admits "M4's send-off gate remains a secondary wiring candidate (granting a long leash is exactly when the trifecta check fires)" — the corpus knows its own gap.
coverage.counter_evidence: The strongest refutation candidate found: curriculum/lectures/module-4-prework.md:30 carries a full by-name treatment ("**Prompt injection** is the name for this surface. Direct prompt injection arrives in the user's input. Indirect prompt injection arrives in a source the agent retrieves and reads...") — but it fails because it belongs to the agents-101 leaders' training (wired only into curriculum/trainings/agents-101/security.md and multi-agent-systems.md), is not in this corpus, and is never wired into any AE101 module. Second candidate: plan-mode-done-right.md:83 contains the words "prompt injection" in student-visible body — but it is a module file outside the corpus, the words describe an external OWASP link, and the section is headed "Optional deeper scan" under a block marked "Optional." Third candidate: when-a-plan-is-good.md:13's read-only plan-mode permission state is an accident model, not an adversary model — the exact split the supplementary itself draws at line 19. No in-room corpus body teaches attacks through the agent under any vocabulary.

materiality.reason: The gap is material by the training's own doctrine, on three grounds. (1) Not out of scope: AE101 spends a full guaranteed in-room module (M3, earn-the-trust) on the classical half of security — access-surface + STRIDE + authoring security skills — and the corpus's own supplementary declares that half insufficient ('STRIDE models attacks on the system; the trifecta models attacks through the agent... A deployment needs both bounds'). theory-plan.md:295 itself rates lethal-trifecta canon-adoption Tier 1 and calls it 'the only true structural hole — agentic adversary model'; line 326 repeats it. The maintainers' own canon-diff concedes this is not completeness-for-its-own-sake. (2) Monday-work failure, not CISO theater: guaranteed in-room content explicitly aims the graduate at the exposed configuration — test-and-learn.md:25 ('Engineers run these for hours at work... ambition and length grow run by run') and the M6 closer's 19.2% no-human-reviewer auto-merge as destination shape — while the one structural fact that bounds that trajectory (instructions-vs-data inseparability: everything the agent reads is a potential instruction) exists only behind three wirings all explicitly labeled 'Optional' (plan-mode-done-right §Pre-reads 'Optional.', earn-the-trust:14 'Optional pre-reads', earn-the-trust:84 'Optional forward-look'). A graduate who takes the training's own encouraged next step — longer leash, connectors with outbound rights, memory and credentials in context — cannot reason from the guaranteed corpus about whether a poisoned issue comment, README, or dependency can steer that run; STRIDE-on-the-feature does not derive it. That is exactly the rubric's CONFIRMED bar. (3) Do-first-name-after is violated in one direction only: the student does the moves (M1 connector = outbound channel, private repo = private data, ambient reads = untrusted content) but the naming is never guaranteed, and the field's one searchable handle, 'prompt injection', is taught nowhere on AE101's guaranteed path and absent even from the supplementary's body — so the durable-anchor doctrine fails for the single security fact the field treats as defining. The fix the finding asks for (one in-room beat at the M4 send-off gate + the term in the trifecta body) is the same wiring the corpus's own maintainer notes already flag as a live candidate.
materiality.counter_evidence: Three of the finding's evidence claims are inflated or false in the letter, though none overturns materiality. (1) ''Prompt injection' appears nowhere in the corpus, supplementary included' is false: the term appears at plan-mode-done-right.md:83 inside the optional OWASP link annotation, and curriculum/lectures/module-4-prework.md:30,35 teaches it fully by name (direct/indirect, mitigation shapes) — but that lecture is wired only into agents-101, not AE101, so the substantive point (never taught on AE101's guaranteed path) stands with a corrected citation. (2) 'M4's multi-hour unattended run' overstates AE101 delivery: walk-and-send-off.md:151 specifies a 15-30 minute same-session run, 'No /schedule, no /loop, no cloud runner'; multi-hour is the explicitly-taught trajectory (test-and-learn.md:25), not the in-room event. (3) 'M6's scheduled sweeps that read repos and open issues' belongs to the agents-101 arc; AE101's M6 exercise (spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md) builds study/gap-finder/verifier skills — the scheduled-agent destination appears in AE101 only as lecture material (19.2% auto-merge, the-loop-has-a-name.md:16). In-training trifecta assembly is therefore legs 1+3 guaranteed (private repo + connector/git push) with leg 2 ambient rather than all three operationally assembled. Also mildly mitigating: the Willison piece is a directed 'Read:' pre-read (10-15 min, timed in earn-the-trust.md:103), so a diligent cohort likely does encounter the frame — the gap is guaranteed exposure, not availability. Severity 'blocking' should be read against these corrections: material and confirmed, but the in-room arc assembles the exposure trajectory, not a live multi-hour unattended trifecta during class hours.

finding.title: Sycophancy/'RLHF' is the training's most repeated watch-for, asserted in module voice across M1-M4, but its mechanism arrives only as one subordinate clause in the M6 memo
finding.evidence: Two lenses converge. Module and lecture bodies lean on the label in their own voice across four modules: 'It flatters. It progresses. It mirrors your stance' (painting-the-picture, M1 — load-bearing for the thesis 'Your stance is the ceiling'); 'The agent agrees easily. A flagged step can come back softened rather than sharpened' (when-a-plan-is-good, M2); 'RLHF softening can dress up a cosmetic edit as a meaningful one' (earn-the-trust); 'LLM self-charity may creep in' (plan-mode-done-right); 'RLHF will frame partial failures as partial successes' (learn-from-the-test); M1's Key Concept 'The agent's self-report is a hypothesis, not ground truth.' Grep of all 27 corpus bodies for RLHF/sycophan/post-training: zero teaching — the only mechanism clause is a one-line subordinate aside at M6 (story-of-module-6: 'Claude produced the credibility framing because its post-training prefers warmth over directness'), landing after three modules have used the label. reading-the-return gives the next-likely-word mechanism for plausible-but-wrong but never the preference-tuning mechanism for flattery. Behavior described, cause hand-waved — exactly the (a)-test a hostile expert runs.
finding.where_it_would_live: One mechanism sentence on curriculum/lectures/painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md's chameleon bullet (post-training optimizes for human approval) or a fourth bullet in reading-the-return.md's 'Why these three, every run' slide.

coverage.reason: Adversarial sweep of all 27 corpus bodies (grep for RLHF, sycophan, post-training, reward, reinforcement, approval, flatter, agreeab, preference, human feedback, trained/tuned to, prefers, warmth, please, praise, bias, optimize, mirror; full reads of painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md, reading-the-return.md, story-of-module-6.md, when-a-plan-is-good.md above their maintainer fences) confirms the finding exactly. The sycophancy label is asserted in module voice with no mechanism: painting-the-picture line 6 'It flatters. It progresses. It mirrors your stance' (load-bearing for line 8 'Your stance is the ceiling'), when-a-plan-is-good line 47 'The agent agrees easily. A flagged step can come back softened rather than sharpened.' The only causal mechanism anywhere in student-visible body is one sentence at story-of-module-6.md line 23 ('...because its post-training prefers warmth over directness'), an M6 war-story aside landing after earlier modules used the label. reading-the-return.md line 18 teaches a mechanism ('statistical machine with a finite window') but scopes it explicitly to the three failure modes (drift/rot/plausible-but-wrong via next-likely-word, line 14) — it never covers the approval/preference-tuning cause of flattery. DOWNGRADE does not apply: the lone M6 sentence is in a guaranteed lecture, and the finding already accounts for it — its claim is late-and-thin mechanism, not absence, and that claim holds.
coverage.counter_evidence: Closest coverage found, neither refuting: (1) reading-the-return.md line 18: 'The LLM is a statistical machine with a finite window: it predicts what is likely, and it holds only so much' — mechanism for the three failure modes, not for flattery; (2) story-of-module-6.md line 23: 'Claude produced the credibility framing because its post-training prefers warmth over directness' — the single M6 clause the finding itself cites as the late arrival. Minor imprecision in the finding: its earn-the-trust / plan-mode-done-right / learn-from-the-test quotes are exercise files outside the 27-file corpus, but the in-corpus label sites (M1 chameleon bullet, M2 when-a-plan-is-good line 47) independently carry the claim.

materiality.reason: Material by the training's own internal bar, not an external PhD bar. (1) The corpus defines theory as "bolded claim + mechanism sentences" and already applied exactly this fix to the three failure modes (reading-the-return.md:12-21, mechanism sentences + the "predictable failures are readable failures" slide, 2026-07-02); sycophancy/softening is the fourth repeated, read-everywhere failure mode — used in module voice at painting-the-picture:6 (M1), push-back-on-the-plan.md:37 ("RLHF doing its job", M2), earn-the-trust.md:62 (M3), learn-from-the-test.md:80 (M5) — and was skipped. (2) The gap is not merely absence; the mechanisms the student DOES get mispredict. Stance-mirroring ("It mirrors your stance... your stance is the ceiling", M1) and next-likely-word (M4) are in-context stories that generate a wrong Monday move: prime it neutrally and the flattery goes away. The missing preference-tuning sentence is what makes priming provably insufficient — i.e., it is the derivation step for the entire far-half claim that you must gate/verify rather than prompt harder, and for M1's Key Concept "self-report is a hypothesis" (currently asserted, not explained — the theory-plan's own named failure mode, theory theatre). A practitioner who re-primes skeptically, still gets a polished report, and concludes the work is good is reasoning correctly from the corpus's available theory and arriving wrong. (3) "RLHF" appears as an undefined acronym in durable student body — the exact anchor-less-material class the cohort complaint that spawned this layer named. Fix cost is one sentence at the finding's proposed M4 home (M1's "don't over-explain the chameleon" watch-for argues against the M1 placement), fully doctrine-compatible: named after it has been felt across M1-M3 exercises.
materiality.counter_evidence: The behavior and its countermeasures ARE taught and enacted repeatedly (over-flag, fresh-session dispatch, check-the-revision at push-back-on-the-plan.md:37/122, quote-verifier-verbatim at learn-from-the-test.md:80), so a student can act correctly in every taught situation without the mechanism — the gap bites only on novel-situation reasoning. Partial mechanisms exist in-corpus: stance-mirroring (painting-the-picture:6-8) and next-likely-word/"fluent and confident is its default finish" (reading-the-return.md:14); the M6 memo clause (story-of-module-6.md:23) IS in the 27-entry theory handbook, so the mechanism technically appears once. reference/prompt-anatomy.md:191,221 carries near-mechanism prose ("Default agent behavior is collaborative-leadership mode"; "The RLHF instinct to please runs deep") but reference/ is outside the theory-handbook manifest (scripts/build-workbook.js:99-141). Audience of shipping ICs plausibly holds folk knowledge of what RLHF is. theory-audit.md:171,184 already catalogs the softening laws as home-grown theory "with a stated mechanism" — but the stated mechanism there is behavioral (revision regresses), not causal, so the audit's own record slightly overstates coverage rather than refuting the gap.

finding.title: The Context-phase law anchor overshoots: 'What the files don't carry, the agent can't act on' is false when quoted alone
finding.evidence: the-loop-half-filled.md:18 — the conditioning repair itself landed exactly as specified ('steer because the agent conditions every move on what they hold', no theorem claim), but the bullet's third sentence absolutizes: 'What the files don't carry, the agent can't act on.' The training's own M1 demonstrates the opposite (how-this-training-was-built.md:28 'You let Claude read your repo') and M4's gap-analysis walks the whole system, not just the files. These slide-form bullets are designed to be quoted later without voice; a staff engineer answers this one with 'the agent greps my codebase all day' and the law anchor loses credibility with it.
finding.where_it_would_live: the-loop-half-filled.md:18, third sentence — scope it to steering (what the files don't carry, the agent must rediscover or guess each session), which is also truer to the bolded claim.

coverage.reason: The third sentence of the-loop-half-filled.md:18 ("What the files don't carry, the agent can't act on") is a false universal that the corpus's own guaranteed-delivery body contradicts three times. (1) the-whole-map.md:96 gives the house-canonical scoped form of this exact law: Context is "the files, rules, and memory that decide whether it acts on your system or on a guess about your system" — the agent acts either way; absent files it acts on a guess. The M3 consolidation absolutizes past what M2 taught. (2) the-agent-loop.md:5+8 (M4, student body) teaches that each iteration the LLM conditions on "system prompt, conversation, tool results" and "keeps choosing the next step from context and tool results" — grep/read results are first-class inputs, so the agent demonstrably acts on things the context files don't carry. (3) how-this-training-was-built.md:28 ("You let Claude read your repo") and where-the-rule-could-live.md:7-9 (agent reads threads/issues/backlogs at run time) show the student living the opposite. The in-bullet scoping defense fails: the file's own Family B artifact contract requires each law bullet be "recoverable from the slide alone" (designed to be quoted without voice, exactly the finding's failure mode), and the maintainer block records the deliberate "conditioning form — no theorem claim" choice that this sentence violates. The finding's proposed fix ("must rediscover or guess each session") matches the corpus's own M2 phrasing.
coverage.counter_evidence: Strongest refutation candidate: the sentence sits inside a bullet whose prior sentence scopes it ("steer because the agent conditions every move on what they hold") and whose closer ("Thin model, thin steering") keeps it in the steering frame. This fails because (a) the sentence upgrades "conditions on what the files hold" to "conditions ONLY on what the files hold," contradicted by the-agent-loop.md:5 ("receives context (system prompt, conversation, tool results)"), and (b) the file's own artifact contract (maintainer line 42) requires each law bullet to stand alone as a quotable slide artifact, so in-bullet context cannot be relied on to rescue it.

materiality.reason: The sentence fails the artifact's own stated bar, not an imported academic one. The file's maintainer block (the-loop-half-filled.md:38) records the design decision to keep the good-regulator law in 'conditioning form — no theorem claim,' and the Family B contract (line 42) requires each law to be 'recoverable from the slide alone' (B★ durability-without-voice); 'What the files don't carry, the agent can't act on' is a theorem-shaped absolute that is false when quoted alone, so it breaks both. Materially, it corrupts the one Monday-work decision the Context phase teaches a practitioner: what must be carried in CLAUDE.md versus what the agent can rediscover by reading the repo — taken literally it prescribes context-file bloat; spotted as false by an audience that already watched Claude read their repo in M1 (how-this-training-was-built.md:28), it costs the law anchor its credibility, which is the exact failure mode the theory layer was built to fix. It also contradicts the sibling anchor the-whole-map.md:96, which carries the correct scoped form ('acts on your system or on a guess about your system'), so the corpus's two map lectures disagree on the Context law. This is a practitioner-reasoning defect in the flagship theory artifact, not PhD completeness.
materiality.counter_evidence: In-room, the misreading is unlikely: the adjacent sentences ('steer because the agent conditions every move on what they hold'; 'Thin model, thin steering') scope the claim to steering, and doctrine (do first, name after) means every student has already lived the agent reading un-carried files in M1. The correct form also exists elsewhere in the corpus (the-whole-map.md:96), so the student's overall model is recoverable. These would support DOWNGRADE only if the bullet were allowed to lean on in-room charity — but the file's own Family B contract explicitly forbids that, which is why the mitigation does not hold.

finding.title: Absorption's entire theory (backpressure, session reach, two ceilings, position change) and the whole far-half governor layer are supplementary-only — in-room the fifth phase is empty and the student exits with exactly one governor
finding.evidence: Six lenses converge. the-far-half.md (in-room M4) stakes Absorption as what 'decides your real throughput' and promises its laws at M5/M6; grep of all 22 in-room lecture bodies: 'absorption' never appears after that opener, and 'backpressure', 'session reach', 'gate designer' appear in zero in-room bodies. Every actual law lives only in supplementary/backpressure.md, an optional M5→M6 pre-read (learn-from-the-test: 'Optional. Skipping does not break Module 6'): human-as-bottleneck ('In the agent loop today, the backpressure is you'), the two-ceilings economics ('The model ceiling moves on its own... finding.evidence: Six lenses converge — the widest agreement in the review. The in-room trust arc only ascends: the-far-half.md:24 'what makes you trust the result at the end?'; what-packaging-is.md:248 'a check trusted enough that the work passing it ships without a human read'; the-loop-has-a-name.md:16 answers with Intercom's 19.2% auto-approved PRs as 'your verifier from M5, scaled', then '...compose. On a pull request. On a nightly run.' Grep of all in-room lecture bodies for complacen|vigilan|goodhart|decay|miscalibrat|hold-out|gamed|tamper|atroph: zero hits. The entire counterweight is wired as M6 exit reading — after the last in-room beat — in two optional supplementaries: the-gate-is-a-claim.md ('Green is a claim about the check, not about the work'; 'A gate nobody has verified is a gate you are trusting on vibes'; 'the agent is an optimizer pointed straight at your gate'; 'One green run is an anecdote with survivorship bias'; Husain's hand-label-and-measure-agreement loop) and ironies-of-automation.md ('the more runs the agent lands cleanly, the worse you get at catching the one that doesn't') — the latter flagged 'Tier-2 cull candidate' by its own status line, though mid-2026 canon (Bainbridge revival, Karpathy on skill atrophy) treats operator decay as a first-class cost. story-of-module-6.md names the condition experientially ('The loop caught what the subagents missed. I caught what the loop missed') but hands over no countermove. finding.title: 'Crossing the wall' — the spine's terminal move — is a mechanism-free label: no promotion criteria, no governor, and no in-room claim about what makes a move count or why wins stay personal by default
finding.evidence: Three lenses converge. the-whole-map.md stakes the claim three times in one artifact: header 'the loop climbs: personal → team → company', soil line 'a move counts when it crosses the wall', and the drawn loop 'CROSSING THE WALL / prove it on yourself → promote' capped 'Trusted shipped change CROSSES TO THE TEAM.' Everything the corpus then teaches about crossing is mechanism, never criterion: skills-from-the-frontier.md 'Promotion to a team home is a human conversation and then a PR, later'; will-company-memory-emerge.md's team gate ('changed only through PR review') and hand-off ('you decide what to call it and where it goes') without a question to decide with; 'Review infrastructure grows by accretion.' Grep for 'promot' across all bodies: no lecture teaches what 'prove it on yourself' must establish before promotion, why walls resist crossing, what makes a personal rule team-true, or when the PR goes up; 'a move counts' appears only in the map, and the sibling soil claim 'competence is the gate' is likewise displayed at M2 and never explained in any file. The audit-sanctioned one-liner (a win stays personal until deliberately institutionalized) is also missing from the M6 closer — the student ends the training having crossed nothing to a team, without the sentence naming that as the default outcome.
finding.where_it_would_live: An Outcome-phase beat at M6 — a promotion-criteria/governor bullet in will-company-memory-emerge.md or the-loop-has-a-name.md (e.g. the-rule-fired-for-a-teammate test), one individual-not-institutional line in the closing slide or kicker, and cash or cut 'competence is the gate'.

'A move counts' and 'competence is the gate' appear ONLY at the-whole-map.md line 92 across the whole corpus, never explained. finding.title: The dead-reckoning analogy over-transfers: 'a fix collapses the wedge to a point' contradicts the corpus's own taught law that checks only see what they watch
finding.evidence: what-packaging-is.md:200 (in-room, M5 closer): 'At a fix the wedge of possible states collapses to a point, and the next leg starts from a known position instead of an assumption.' A navigational fix measures all of position; a test-pass measures a projection. The corpus itself says so twice: the-loop-half-filled.md:17 (in-room, M3) 'The drift lives in what nothing is watching: the dimensions your tests and reads don't cover', and the-gate-is-a-claim.md:5 'A gate only means what the gate can see.' A staff engineer holding M3's law will correctly reject the M5 chart's claim — or worse, believe it and treat any green check as a full position fix, which is precisely the failure the-gate-is-a-claim exists to prevent.
finding.where_it_would_live: what-packaging-is.md chart bullet at line 200 (and the SVG label 'position known again. the wedge collapses.'): reword to collapse-along-the-dimensions-the-check-sees, which also seeds the gate-validity condition in-room.

coverage.reason: The M5 in-room chart bullet (curriculum/lectures/what-packaging-is.md:200) asserts full-state collapse — "At a fix the wedge of possible states collapses to a point, and the next leg starts from a known position instead of an assumption" — and the lecture generalizes this beyond the human diagnose: line 199 defines the wedge as "everywhere the run might be," line 204 extends fix-semantics to checks that "stand without you," and line 211 defines those checks as quality-bar decisions (projections). The corpus even supplies its own in-room counterexample: story-of-module-6.md recounts a verifier loop that "stopped clean" while ten defects remained ("The loop caught what the subagents missed. I caught what the loop missed."). Both colliding texts are in-room, so the contradiction is guaranteed-delivery vs guaranteed-delivery; severity major stands.

The reconciling law exists only in the optional supplementary (the-gate-is-a-claim.md:5, "A gate only means what the gate can see"), which cannot repair an in-room contradiction.

finding.title: Economics is one bullet deep: the 'ROI calculation' is invoked but never shown, and standing/scheduled agents are recommended with no cost dimension

finding.title: 'Taste closes the gap. Nothing else does' contradicts the far half's central teaching — and the module the memo opens — that engineered gates close the gap without you
finding.evidence: Two lenses converge. story-of-module-6.md's bolded generalisation: 'A rule in context is not a rule in the output. Taste closes the gap. Nothing else does.' The LLM self-check did not' and 'I moved them into skills that block "done" until the check runs' — a forcing function, not taste, closing the gap. The module the memo opens then teaches that automated checks are what close the rule-to-output gap at scale (the-loop-has-a-name's eval thesis), and the corpus's own backpressure supplementary quotes da Costa: 'Any system that relies on a human to catch the machine's mistakes will be limited by the human' plus ironies-of-automation's 'Let the gates carry the vigilance... automated checks do not get complacent.' The reconciliation exists (taste decides what becomes a gate; the gate then holds it without you) but no file makes it — and the absolute is the last theory statement the student hears before building their second gate.
finding.where_it_would_live: curriculum/lectures/story-of-module-6.md § The generalisation — one sentence turning the absolute into the division of labor: taste catches the un-encoded and decides what to encode; the encoded check holds it without you.
