The absolute at line 45 concludes a paragraph (line 43) that explicitly credits the engineered loop with catching failures, so "nothing else" targets the LLM's self-application ("The grep pass caught each one. The LLM self-check did not"), not gates. The next section (line 49) performs the taste-becomes-gate move verbatim, and the memo's actual closing theory statement (line 61) — which, contra the finding, is the last theory the student hears before the exercise, not the absolute — states the division of labor directly. Corroborated by guaranteed in-room M5 coverage in what-packaging-is.md line 226 (taste-shaped vs must-happen/hook division). The contradiction reading requires quoting line 45 out of its immediate context; a linear reader gets loop-catches → taste-catches-what-loop-missed → encode-into-forcing-skills → "catch it and encode what you learned." DOWNGRADE does not apply: coverage is in-room body, not supplementary-only.
coverage.counter_evidence: story-of-module-6.md, student-visible body: line 43 (immediately before the absolute): "The loop caught what the subagents missed. I caught what the loop missed. You will catch what I missed." Line 49 (the section right after): "The diff found rules that lived in memory but never fired. I moved them into skills that block 'done' until the check runs. A rule in memory that does not force is worse than no rule." Line 61 (the memo's closing theory statement, after the absolute): "Your job is not to stop the drift. Your job is to catch it and encode what you learned." Line 63: "I fixed what I caught. The loop caught what I missed. A senior-persona sim caught what the loop missed." Plus in-room M5 lecture what-packaging-is.md line 226: "Must happen goes in a hook; recommended stays in a prompt or rule... Anything taste-shaped or context-dependent stays in a prompt where the LLM weighs it. Hooks are the runtime's 'I will not forget.'"

materiality.reason: The contradiction is real, student-facing, and sits on the training's central practical question rather than a completeness nicety. story-of-module-6.md:45 ('Taste closes the gap. Nothing else does.') is the closing generalisation of the M6 opener, delivered before the student builds their second gate — and it is contradicted by the memo's own evidence two paragraphs away ('The grep pass caught each one. The LLM self-check did not'; 'I moved them into skills that block done until the check runs'), by the module's Key Concepts and closer (the-loop-has-a-name: evals as the automated check that holds the bar; Intercom's 19.2% no-human merges), and by the corpus's own doctrine files (backpressure.md's da Costa human-bottleneck line; ironies-of-automation's 'Let the gates carry the vigilance'). Decisively, the maintainer's own internal arc note (curriculum/module-design/ae101-m6-gen-arc-note.md:45-58) already concedes the line 'names half the truth' and that the reconciling claim 'is not yet named in any file the student reads' — a documented, unfixed gap, not reviewer invention. Materiality: the taste-vs-gates division of labor IS the far half's Monday-work question (drift appears — review harder, or encode a check?). A student who anchors on the absolute resolves drift with more human vigilance, the exact anti-pattern M4-M6 exist to dismantle; a careful student sees two opposite-pointing claims in the same module with no resolution anywhere in the corpus. Either way, understanding — the review's stated bar — is damaged at the training's climax. The fix is the one sentence the finding names, but the gap itself is practitioner-material, not syllabus polish.
materiality.counter_evidence: Three softeners, none sufficient to downgrade: (1) The finding overstates one detail — the line is NOT bolded in story-of-module-6.md; it is the plain closing line of a section titled 'The generalisation' (the memo's actual bolded text is elsewhere). (2) The memo's register is first-person memoir explicitly marked 'permission-giving, not credibility-performance', and its own narrative displays the layered cascade ('The loop caught what the subagents missed. I caught what the loop missed'), so a generous reader can assemble the reconciliation unaided. (3) Per the 'do first, name after' doctrine, the student spends the exercise building a gate that demonstrably closes a gap, and then hears the closer name it — experiential teaching partially self-corrects the opener's absolute. But the theory layer under review exists precisely because experience-without-articulation drew the 'too little theory' complaint; judged as anchors-that-carry-understanding, the absolute still points the wrong way and no student-facing file corrects it.

finding.title: 'Drift' wears three different mechanisms across M3-M5 (unobserved dimensions, buried intent, compounding error between checks) and the corpus never equates or distinguishes them
finding.evidence: Three drifts, all guaranteed surfaces: (1) the-loop-half-filled.md 'Local success, global drift... The drift lives in what nothing is watching' (unobserved dimensions); (2) reading-the-return.md 'Goal drift. The instructions get buried... the agent is solving an adjacent problem' (buried intent); (3) what-packaging-is.md's chart 'Drift grows with distance since the last check. The agent steers each step from its own previous step, so small errors compound silently' (compounding error between checks — the mechanism whose fix is checks, i.e. the 0.85ⁿ chain law). The same slide then prescribes 'Reference artefact, against goal drift', so the chart's drift wedge and the failure mode it allegedly fixes are different mechanisms wearing one word, two inches apart. A careful student cannot say whether the reference fixes chart-drift, whether global drift IS goal drift, or which drift the verifier resets. No passage anywhere equates or separates the three.
finding.where_it_would_live: One disambiguation bullet in what-packaging-is.md's chart slide (chart-drift = compounding error, reset by checks; goal drift = buried intent, anchored by the reference) plus a clause in the-loop-half-filled.md distinguishing global drift from goal drift when the latter arrives at M4.

coverage.reason: The finding's predicted confusions are each answered in guaranteed student-visible body. (1) "Which drift the verifier resets" — the corpus explicitly assigns the verifier to plausible-but-wrong, not drift, with an exclusive one-each mapping. (2) "Whether the reference fixes chart-drift" — the reference bullet restates goal drift's mechanism (buried instructions) and its fix in the same sentence; 'goal drift' is never the bare word anywhere, it carries its buried-instructions definition at all three occurrences (reading-the-return L12, learning-through-contrast L13, what-packaging-is L209). (3) The "compounding error between checks" mechanism is not an M5-only third sense: the finder missed the-loop-half-filled.md L16, directly adjacent to the line they quoted, which introduces drift-as-loss-of-feedback-signal ("a test, a check, a read") and ties it to L17's unwatched-dimensions drift through shared vocabulary ("the dimensions your tests and reads don't cover"). The M5 chart wedge ("everywhere the run might be", collapsed when "something outside the run measures position") is the same feedback-measurement frame drawn geometrically, and learning-through-contrast L23 closes the loop by naming reference/plan/check together as what "the agent re-anchors from." The residual wish — one explicit chart-drift-vs-goal-drift meta-sentence — is a polish preference, not a major missing-theory gap; the headline claim "no passage anywhere equates or separates the three" is contradicted by the text.
coverage.counter_evidence: what-packaging-is.md L209-213 (student-visible body): "**Reference artefact, against goal drift.** A spec the agent reads and re-reads... The spec on disk stays readable mid-run when the buried instructions in the conversation no longer are." / "**External verifier, against plausible-but-wrong.**" / "Three failures you named, three pieces, one each." — the fix↔failure mapping is explicit and exclusive. the-loop-half-filled.md L16-17 (student-visible body, adjacent bullets): "Cut the feedback signal (a test, a check, a read) and it drifts. Signal quality is part of the law: a flaky test is a closed loop that still drifts." / "The drift lives in what nothing is watching: the dimensions your tests and reads don't cover." — M3 already unifies compounding-without-checks with unwatched-dimensions under the one feedback-signal frame, the same frame the M5 chart draws: "small errors compound silently until something outside the run measures position. On the chart that is the wedge: everywhere the run might be." (what-packaging-is.md L199). learning-through-contrast.md L23: "give the agent something durable to re-read... Once those exist, auto-compact can fire and the agent re-anchors from what survives." — reference/plan/check named together as re-anchors against the accumulating-drift mechanism.

materiality.reason: The lexical overload is real, but it never blocks a practitioner's Monday reasoning: every occurrence of "drift" co-locates its mechanism with its fix in the same bullet (M3 global-drift → second read; goal drift → reference, with the buried-instructions mechanism restated at the mapping site; chart drift → "a check is a position fix"), and the authoritative failure→piece map is stated outright ("Three failures you named, three pieces, one each", what-packaging-is.md:213). The only disambiguation with operational stakes — goal drift vs context rot vs plausible-but-wrong — is already enforced as an enacted governor (learn-from-the-test.md:132 names failure-mode collapse and forces distinct quoted moments per lens). Conflating the three drifts changes no action: all converge on "something external must re-anchor the run." What remains is a generic English word used generically plus one coined term of art, and the demand to formally cross-walk them is textbook-completeness the training's doctrine explicitly rejects. The finding's own remedy (one bullet + one clause) sizes it as polish, not a major spine gap. Minor, not major; worth the cheap fix in what-packaging-is.md where the two senses sit two inches apart, but not a comprehension blocker.
materiality.counter_evidence: (1) what-packaging-is.md:199-213 — the chart slide self-defines its drift and its fix ("A check is a position fix"), then the three-pattern slide gives the explicit one-each mapping, so "which piece fixes which failure" is answered on the same page the finding claims is ambiguous. (2) the-loop-half-filled.md:17 — the global-drift bullet carries its own fix inline ("That is why the second read exists"), so a student cannot grab the reference artefact for unwatched-dimensions drift without skipping the sentence that names the actual remedy. (3) learn-from-the-test.md:123-132 + :150 — the M5 exercise already forces per-lens separation with quoted moments and names lens-conflation as a trainer watch-for, i.e. the corpus operationalizes drift-disambiguation at the grain that matters for practice. (4) reading-the-return.md:12-21 — goal drift arrives with mechanism and diagnostic attached ("compare what was asked against what was done"), independent of any other use of the word.

finding.title: The two-run arc credits packaging on n=1 — exactly what the-gate-is-a-claim forbids — with no reconciliation anywhere
finding.evidence: The core M4-M5 pedagogy: diagnose one un-packaged run, build the packaging, re-send once, credit the packaging — what-packaging-is.md: 'without it you paid for a run that missed the goal, with it you paid for a run that landed.' the-gate-is-a-claim.md (M6-linked supplementary): 'Before crediting an improvement, run it repeatedly... On one run you cannot tell the change from the run-to-run variance', 'One stochastic miss is not a process failure... React on recurrence', plus Deming's tampering. A good student who reads the supplementary finds the training's own signature move classified as tampering-plus-survivorship-bias. The defensible reconciliation — a diagnosed structural mechanism (quoted moment + named failure mode) justifies an immediate fix; an undiagnosed miss waits for recurrence — is never stated anywhere.
finding.where_it_would_live: the-gate-is-a-claim.md § Change the process on recurrence — one bullet distinguishing mechanism-diagnosed fixes (the M5 move) from noise-chasing; mirrored in a clause on what-packaging-is.md's 80/20 slide.

coverage.reason: The claimed contradiction requires ignoring the scoping language both texts carry in student-visible body. the-gate-is-a-claim.md's tampering law explicitly targets undiagnosed noise-chasing ("occasional misses even when nothing is wrong", "rewriting a rule after every single miss") — not the M5 move, which the guaranteed lectures gate on diagnosis: learning-through-contrast.md (in-room M5 opener) teaches exactly the anti-tampering stance ("Diagnose first. Fix later... holds that reflex off. The failures earn the validation that catches them"), and reading-the-return.md (dual-wired M4-close pre-read + M5-open re-projection) states both halves of the "missing" reconciliation — the fix targets structural mechanisms, not variance ("The three are consequences, not bad luck... Run it long enough and all three failures follow"), with field-wide recurrence already established ("the same three turn up in every practitioner's long run"), entered only via the quoted-moment + named-mechanism bar ("A finding counts when it carries a lens and a quoted moment from the artefact"). Nor does the arc credit packaging as n=1 proof: guaranteed M6 lectures immediately re-diagnose the packaged run (story-of-module-6.md: "diagnose gaps in the M5 packaged run"; "every run surprises you, even when nothing about the surface changed") and quality-is-grounding.md packages the packaged run's "dominant gap". The what-packaging-is.md "run that landed" clause is an ROI illustration of the felt contrast (test-and-learn.md: "The contrast is the lesson"), not a reliability certification the corpus builds on. A one-clause hedge on the 80/20 slide would be polish, not the claimed major unreconciled contradiction.
coverage.counter_evidence: the-gate-is-a-claim.md §Change the process on recurrence (self-scoping): "One stochastic miss is not a process failure. A system with run-to-run variance produces occasional misses even when nothing is wrong. Rewriting a rule after every single miss does not tighten the process; it churns it." — vs the guaranteed-delivery diagnosis gate: learning-through-contrast.md §Diagnose first, fix later: "When an agent gets something wrong, the reflex is to fix it immediately: edit the prompt, add a constraint, reach for the next tool. This module holds that reflex off. The failures earn the validation that catches them." — and the structural-mechanism/recurrence half, reading-the-return.md §Why these three, every run: "The three are consequences, not bad luck. The LLM is a statistical machine with a finite window... Run it long enough and all three failures follow, whoever wrote the prompt." / "Because the three come from the machine, the same three turn up in every practitioner's long run." / "A finding counts when it carries a lens and a quoted moment from the artefact." — and the no-n=1-proof half, story-of-module-6.md §What the loop is for: "The next hour you will diagnose gaps in the M5 packaged run... A rule you wrote last module will not fire this run... The loop exists because every run surprises you, even when nothing about the surface changed."

materiality.reason: This is a contradiction on the trigger condition of the training's own central loop, and it fires on the practitioner's most common Monday event: a run misses — edit the rule/packaging now, or wait for recurrence? what-packaging-is.md line 243 credits packaging causally on the single run that followed the change ("with it you paid for a run that landed"), while the-gate-is-a-claim.md lines 24/29-31 forbid exactly that ("judge it on pass rates across several runs, not on the single run that followed the change"; Deming tampering; and a regression-to-the-mean bullet — "a tweak made right after a failure looks effective even when it did nothing" — that describes the M5 re-send verbatim). The supplementary is in-scope by the training's own design: its maintainer block names dont-tamper as "one of the 8 confirmed delivery gaps, delivered here," and it is wired from M6 ## Next. Grep across all lectures, modules, supplementaries, and the new theory files (the-far-half, the-whole-map, the-loop-half-filled, the-2-frontiers) confirms the reconciling discriminator — mechanism-diagnosed fix acts now, undiagnosed miss waits for recurrence — is stated nowhere, even though the M5 exercise (diagnose-and-resend.md: quoted moments + named failure modes; "the un-packaged run was supposed to underdeliver") makes the arc defensible as Deming special-cause action in one sentence. A student who reads the corpus as one document either concludes the signature M4-M5 arc was tampering-plus-survivorship-bias (trust in the loop damaged) or fails to transfer dont-tamper to their own post-failure tweaks (the law doesn't land). Both outcomes leave the practitioner unable to reason about the fix-now-vs-wait decision they face weekly — squarely the CONFIRMED bar, not textbook completeness. The training's do-first-name-after doctrine makes it worse, not better: the doing is sound, but the naming half actively mis-names the doing as a forbidden move.
materiality.counter_evidence: The corpus partially hedges the n=1 credit: the M5 trainer push says "the point is to read the contrast at M6, not to nail it this run" (learn-from-the-test.md line 128), and M6's Key Concepts frame the two runs as "data the pattern didn't anticipate, not proof the pattern failed" (spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md line 44) — the two-run arc is pitched as diagnostic contrast, not a controlled experiment. The M5 fix is mechanism-diagnosed (quoted moments + three named failure modes), which is exactly the special-cause action Deming permits, so a sharp student could self-reconcile; and the collision only manifests head-on for students who read the optional M6-linked supplementary. These hedges lower severity but do not supply the missing discriminator: they address whether run 2 should be expected to succeed, not when a single diagnosed miss licenses a process change — and the unhedged causal ROI line remains in-room M5 core content.

finding.title: Worktree / parallel-session theory exists only in optional pre-reads; M3 requires the move and M5's whole structure stands on it
finding.evidence: earn-the-trust LO #1: 'Fork a sibling git worktree and run two Claude Code sessions on the same history side by side, one window per lane.' learn-from-the-test builds M5 on the fork and admits the dependency on an optional artifact: 'Cherny's productivity unlock from the Module 2 pre-read lands here' — that pre-read (Cherny video + multi-session-git reference page) is explicitly optional at M1/M2. Grep for 'worktree' in corpus bodies: only steering-the-wiring (M6), which post-hoc theorizes the fork's gitignore wiring assuming the primitive is already understood. The why (parallel sessions without clobbering state, one window per lane, session-per-lane concurrency) has zero guaranteed theory home despite being invoked in three modules.
finding.where_it_would_live: A short worktree/parallel-sessions beat at M3 open (alongside the open-the-side-quest exercise) or a Work-phase bullet in curriculum/lectures/the-whole-map.md; minimally promote the multi-session-git reference content into a lecture.

coverage.reason: Exhaustive greps (worktree, fork, sibling, lane, parallel, side-by-side, clobber, two sessions, concurrent, same history, side quest, at once) across all 22 corpus lectures and 5 supplementaries found zero guaranteed-delivery theory for the worktree/parallel-session primitive. The only student-visible corpus mention is steering-the-wiring.md (M6), which opens 'The worktree fork at M5 ran on one wiring' and theorizes the gitignore/cp wiring post-hoc — it presupposes the primitive, it does not teach it. Meanwhile earn-the-trust.md (M3) LO #1 requires the move verbatim ('Fork a sibling git worktree and run two Claude Code sessions on the same history side by side, one window per lane'), learn-from-the-test.md (M5) builds its whole structure on the fork and explicitly leans on the optional artifact ('Cherny's productivity unlock from the Module 2 pre-read lands here'), and getting-going.md line 50 confirms that pre-read (Cherny video + multi-session-git reference page) is labeled 'Optional.' The why of parallel sessions has no guaranteed theory home despite being load-bearing in three modules — exactly as the finding claims. Not a DOWNGRADE: the coverage isn't even in a listed supplementary, only in an optional pre-read outside the corpus.
coverage.counter_evidence: Closest corpus coverage: curriculum/lectures/steering-the-wiring.md (M6) student body — 'The worktree fork at M5 ran on one wiring. It's not the only one. ... Gitignored files don't ride into a worktree. Git doesn't see CLAUDE.local.md or observations/, so the M5 fork of the M4 starting SHA forced a real engineering call.' This is guaranteed delivery but arrives three modules after M3 first requires the move and assumes the student already understands the primitive; it covers fork wiring, not session-per-lane concurrency or the why of parallel sessions. Module bodies (earn-the-trust LO, learn-from-the-test fork section) carry operational rationale, but those are exercise/module mechanics, not the theory corpus under judgment.

materiality.reason: The claimed gap — "zero guaranteed theory home" for worktree/parallel-session reasoning — is factually wrong. The why is taught in the guaranteed path at both use sites, exactly where this training's doctrine (do first, name after; theory at point of use, not a lecture) says it should live. (1) M3's mandatory opener exercise `curriculum/exercises/open-the-side-quest.md` (inlined into `earn-the-trust.md` § Start here) carries the primitive's theory in-body: what a sibling worktree is, why ("two unrelated changes need to run concurrently, each on its own branch and working state"), the mechanism (same git history, independent working state, CLAUDE.local.md doesn't sync, copied once at fork), the one-session-per-working-directory clobber rule (push-back move sourced to multi-session-git.md), and the concurrency move being installed ("when one window kicks off a long-running prompt, the other is where you work"). It also links the multi-session-git reference at the exact point of need — so the reference is not reachable only via the optional M1/M2 pre-read. (2) M5's `learn-from-the-test.md` § Set up the Module 5 worktree stands alone: it states the fork's purpose in-body (second run starts from the same code state as the first) and walks the gitignored-files engineering call (copy at fork, diverge, merge-back decision post-M6). The "Cherny's productivity unlock from the Module 2 pre-read lands here" line is attribution garnish for students who did the pre-read, not a load-bearing dependency — a student who skipped it loses nothing needed to execute or reason about M5. (3) M6's `steering-the-wiring.md` then names the wiring post-hoc, which is the doctrine working as designed, not evidence of a hole. As for the demand that the worktree beat live in `the-whole-map.md`: worktrees are a git delivery mechanic, not a phase of the cycle; the theory layer's own plan (theory-plan.md) covers parallelism's governor at the right altitude via the starred absorption-bottleneck law, and the map lecture's container discipline explicitly forbids teaching mechanics there. A practitioner exits able to reason about Monday parallel-session work — the M3 exercise even carries a falsifiable Monday leap test for exactly this behavior (opens a sibling worktree when two changes need to run concurrently; one window per worktree; routes work by lane).
materiality.counter_evidence: curriculum/exercises/open-the-side-quest.md lines 5–7 and 35 (guaranteed M3 path, inlined via earn-the-trust.md line 33): "A sibling worktree is a second working directory on the same git history... Useful when two unrelated changes need to run concurrently, each on its own branch and working state... both sessions see the same git history but keep their own working state. Personal files like CLAUDE.local.md don't sync between worktrees" + "The move you're installing this module: when one window kicks off a long-running prompt, the other is where you work." Same file links the multi-session-git reference in-body ("carries the longer read") and its push-back moves teach the clobber rule ("the later write wins; the earlier session goes sideways"). learn-from-the-test.md lines 22–24 (guaranteed M5 path): fork rationale stated in-body ("so the second run starts from the same code state as the first") plus the full gitignored-files engineering call — self-sufficient without the optional pre-read. The finding's own grep ("only steering-the-wiring") missed both because it looked only at lecture bodies, while the training's doctrine locates this theory in exercise/module bodies at the moment of use.

finding.title: Frozen weights / no learning between sessions is never stated — the foundation of the entire memory-in-files thesis is assumed, not taught
finding.evidence: The whole training rests on: the model cannot learn from use, therefore all learning must live in files the next session reads. That premise is never stated. Closest approaches: 'context = the live window... the part that empties when the session ends. Memory is what survives' (will-company-memory-emerge.md — describes the effect, not the cause) and 'The kit compounds; the model rotates' (agents-that-build-agents.md — assumes it). The word 'weights' appears nowhere in the corpus; no lecture explains why Claude won't remember Tuesday's correction on Wednesday. Even the arc's framing question — 'How can a system like this learn faster than a human practitioner can write things down?' (painting-the-picture, the-2-frontiers) — hides its own precondition: writing things down is the ONLY channel this system learns through. One bullet would ground the compound ladder, CLAUDE.md, skills, and the two frontiers in a mechanism instead of a practice.
finding.where_it_would_live: One bullet in curriculum/lectures/the-agent-loop.md's anatomy slide (the model is frozen; everything it 'learns' from you lives in the window or on disk) or in painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md's two-frontiers slide.

coverage.reason: The finding's headline — "no learning between sessions is never stated" — is contradicted by guaranteed in-room student-visible body the finder missed. The M6 closer (the-loop-has-a-name.md, above the fence) grounds each practice in an explicit because-chain including cross-session non-persistence ("a lesson learned once and not written down gets learned again next week" — unambiguously week-to-week, separate from the window-fills clause), which is exactly the one bullet the finding claims doesn't exist. The corpus further grounds memory-in-files in a stated mechanism the finding dismisses as assumption: model transience ("The specific Claude you used today will be replaced, probably within months"; "The kit compounds; the model rotates... the model underneath gets replaced every few months"), which entails files-carry-learning even without the weights premise. The M4 closer states the persistence split ("the live window... empties when the session ends. Memory is what survives"). What is literally absent is only the word "weights"/the neural mechanism — but the training's declared doctrine is name-after ("You do the moves first and name them after") and "WHY, not the math of HOW," so the missing neural vocabulary is a depth choice, not an untaught foundation. The thesis is grounded in stated system properties in guaranteed lectures, twice.
coverage.counter_evidence: curriculum/lectures/the-loop-has-a-name.md (M6 in-room closer, lines 28–29, above fence): "The specific Claude you used today will be replaced, probably within months... Reference because the agent forgets. Plan because the window fills. Verifier because plausible-but-wrong is the default failure mode of a statistical machine. Encode because a lesson learned once and not written down gets learned again next week." Also curriculum/lectures/agents-that-build-agents.md line 41 (above fence at 45): "The kit compounds; the model rotates. The kit grows, the rules sharpen, the skills accumulate, and the model underneath gets replaced every few months without changing the move." Also curriculum/lectures/will-company-memory-emerge.md lines 3+7 (M4 in-room closer): "The persistent part of what you walked, the rules and notes and skills that outlast the session, is what this training calls memory... context already names the live window the agent holds while it works, the part that empties when the session ends. Memory is what survives."

materiality.reason: The gap is real at the causal layer ("weights"/"frozen" genuinely appear nowhere in the corpus) but the finding's severity claim — "the foundation of the entire memory-in-files thesis is assumed, not taught" — is wrong. The foundation IS taught, repeatedly, in AE101 student body, at the effect level that actually governs moves: nothing survives the session except what's on disk. What's missing is only the substrate-level cause one layer down (weights fixed at training time, inference is read-only). Apply the training's own materiality test: every Monday decision the frozen-weights premise would generate — put the correction in CLAUDE.md not the next prompt, encode after the run, re-read files at session boot, hook what must not be forgotten — is already generated by the effect-level anchors the corpus ships in M1, M4, M5, and M6. A practitioner asked "why doesn't Claude remember Tuesday's correction?" can answer defensibly from the corpus: the window empties at session end; memory is what survives; a lesson not written down gets learned again next week. That is a correct, move-generating answer; the frozen-weights bullet adds explanatory closure, not capability. Two further deflators: (a) the crisp bullet as proposed would immediately need nuance to stay true — harness-level memory (Claude Code's auto-memory at ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/, surfaced via /memory, which AE101's own training-architecture.md documents) DOES persist things across sessions without user-authored files, and in-context learning does happen within a session — so the file-and-window framing the corpus uses is arguably the more operationally honest level, not an omission; (b) by the training's own 37-law bank (theory-plan.md inventory sections 0–6), frozen-weights was never a banked law that failed to ship — the finding is proposing new theory, and the doctrine (theory as durable anchors that generate moves, ≤3 laws/module, WHY not the math of HOW) does not owe substrate mechanism where the effect-law already pays full rent. The one place the cause could matter for reasoning (does this discipline survive a model that learns?) is already argued on independent grounds in M6's the-loop-has-a-name § "Why the loop survives the model". Verdict: worth the one proposed bullet as polish — it would ground the compound ladder cheaply for an engineer audience — but a minor completeness improvement, not a major hole that blocks understanding.
materiality.counter_evidence: AE101 student-facing body already states the operative law in four of six modules: M1 curriculum/lectures/the-wizard-move.md:27 — "Seed a rules file your next session reads first"; M4 curriculum/lectures/will-company-memory-emerge.md:7 — "context already names the live window the agent holds while it works, the part that empties when the session ends. Memory is what survives" (the finding cites this but undersells it — it is a mechanism statement at the window/disk level, the level the student operates at); M5 curriculum/lectures/what-packaging-is.md:210,224-226 — "What got ruled out an hour ago is written down, not remembered... Hooks exist because the LLM is forgetful... The runtime fires the script whether or not the model remembers it exists"; M6 curriculum/lectures/the-loop-has-a-name.md:29 — "Reference because the agent forgets. Plan because the window fills. Verifier because plausible-but-wrong is the default failure mode of a statistical machine. Encode because a lesson learned once and not written down gets learned again next week" — a four-clause causal statement tying every artifact of the system to the machine's non-retention. M1's exercise (curriculum/exercises/compound-and-close.md:18) enacts it: "The rules file is yours, born from the session, extended by every module after this one." Additionally, the strict form of the proposed bullet ("the model is frozen; everything it learns lives in the window or on disk") is complicated by the corpus's own documented platform reality: curriculum/trainings/agentic-engineering-101/training-architecture.md:69 documents Claude Code's auto-memory namespace persisting agent recall across sessions without user files — so the effect-level framing is not an evasion of the mechanism, it is the accurate teaching level.

minors.title: The M2 map's drawn loop-strand vocabulary (CONTEXT/PLAN/SECURITY/PROTOTYPING/BUILD/CHECKING/COMPOUNDING loops, CROSSING THE WALL) is shown once and never earned, re-used, or filled — breaking the corpus's own name-after doctrine
minors.evidence: Two lenses converge. the-whole-map.md SVG (lines 54-62) projects the strand names under the doctrine 'You do the moves first and name them after.' Grep across all other corpus bodies: none of these loop names recurs anywhere (only 'crosses the wall' gets a single echo in the-far-half.md:18). The near-half close names its laws with entirely different vocabulary (closed-loop controller, local success/global drift, compound ladder), and the M3/M4 ASCII re-reveals show phases only, so the loop layer is single-exposure furniture. Two strands promise content the corpus never delivers in-room: THE PROTOTYPING LOOP ('spin a throwaway to finish the plan') has no corresponding move anywhere in the training, and THE SECURITY LOOP's agent-side content never materializes. The checking loop's caption 'read · judge · gate what ships · by hand for now' promises a no-longer-by-hand callback; when the student builds exactly that at M5-M6, the-loop-has-a-name.md names it eval without ever saying this is the map's checking loop, now built. Names delivered before any move and then abandoned.
minors.where_it_would_live: curriculum/lectures/the-whole-map.md — trim the strands to the ones later lectures actually fill, or add callback lines in the two consolidation closers (the-loop-half-filled.md: plan/context/security/build loops = the moves just lived; the-loop-has-a-name.md: eval = the checking loop, built).

minors.title: Named concepts with no reconstructable corpus home: closers lean on exercise-only vocabulary (worktree fork, three-block memory, session-shaper, Phase 3/4) and module LOs name concepts the handbook never defines (Huryn's three blocks, approval inflation, 'at least 10% wrong')
minors.evidence: Two lenses converge from opposite directions on the same gap. Lecture side: steering-the-wiring opens 'The worktree fork at M5 ran on one wiring' and recaps 'worktrees, reference + plan + verifier, compound loops, three-block memory' — grep shows worktree/three-block appear in no other corpus body (6 hits, all this file) and neither is defined in a lecture; the-loop-has-a-name anchors its earning slide on 'the session-shaper's shape', a name defined nowhere; what-packaging-is refers to 'the reference you assembled at Phase 4' / 'You picked one at Phase 3.' Module side: run-the-first-experiment's LO 'Name the three blocks in your own material: Block 1 (observation → hypothesis → rule), Block 2 (decisions + alternatives), Block 3 (quality criteria)' — grep for 'three block' across all corpus bodies: zero; M2's LO 'Spot approval inflation' and Key Concept 'Remember: at least 10% wrong' — neither the label nor the quantifier appears anywhere in the corpus. All are exercise-memory dependencies; months later these are the beats most likely to have faded, exactly where the graduation and eval-naming arguments hang, and a student re-reading the theory handbook cannot find the named concepts.
minors.where_it_would_live: One reconstructing clause at first use in each closer (e.g. 'the worktree — the parallel checkout M5 forked so the re-send ran clean'); a three-blocks bullet in test-and-learn.md or will-company-memory-emerge.md; the approval-inflation label (and the 10% mnemonic's grounding or removal) in when-a-plan-is-good.md's three-pressures slide.

minors.title: The practitioner's middle-altitude loop wears four step-decompositions (plan-work-review-compound / orient-act-verify-compound / test→learn→encode / diff-name-package) with no equation
minors.evidence: M1 how-this-training-was-built.md: 'his loop: plan, work, review, compound' (named as the pattern the student just ran). M3 the-loop-half-filled.md: 'Your cycle around each run: orient, act, verify, compound.' M5 learning-through-contrast.md: 'The arc is test → learn → encode.' M6 the-loop-has-a-name.md: 'the encode loop you ran at M6 (diff, name the gaps, package the learning).' The corpus's own doctrine says 'A name is a handle... transferable to a teammate' — but a student asked to state their own loop has four unreconciled handles for what M3 insists is 'one loop, three sizes' (all four sit at the same middle altitude). No passage equates orient-act-verify-compound with Klaassen's four steps or places test→learn→encode on the size ladder.
minors.where_it_would_live: One equation clause in the-loop-half-filled.md's 'one loop, three sizes' bullet (your cycle = Klaassen's plan-work-review-compound in this training's words) and one in learning-through-contrast.md's arc bullet (test→learn→encode = the same cycle stretched across three modules).

minors.title: M1's 'rules became load-bearing / every session got better' is contradicted by M6's 'a rule in context is not a rule in the output' without the pair ever being reconciled
minors.evidence: how-this-training-was-built.md (M1): 'Rules in a file Claude Code could read stopped being reminders and became load-bearing' and 'Every session inherited what the last one figured out. Every session got better.' story-of-module-6.md (M6): 'The rules were loaded every time... Claude typed the banned word anyway' and 'A rule in context is not a rule in the output. Taste closes the gap.' M6 supplies the repair (rules that never fired moved 'into skills that block "done" until the check runs') but never marks M1's claim as the half-truth it corrects — a careful student holds 'loaded rules are load-bearing' and 'loaded rules leak' as a live conflict, since neither passage cites the other.
minors.where_it_would_live: One sentence in story-of-module-6.md's generalisation section explicitly amending the M1 claim (rules are load-bearing AND leaky; forcing functions close the gap the file alone leaves open).

minors.title: 'That is the design, not a flaw' asserts sycophancy as intended design with no ground offered — contradicted by the corpus's own M6 treatment of flattery as a defect
minors.evidence: painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md:6-8: 'It flatters. It progresses. It mirrors your stance... That is the design, not a flaw.' Stance-conditioning is design; flattery is a known post-training artifact that labs actively mitigate — the corpus's own M6 lecture treats it as a defect (story-of-module-6.md:24 'Claude produced the credibility framing because its post-training prefers warmth over directness', presented as a failure to catch). A student re-quoting 'the flattery is the design' is saying something the field, and this corpus's own M6, contradicts; no mechanism for the flattery half is ever supplied. (Distinct from the missing-mechanism finding on sycophancy: this is the specific false/overreaching design claim.)
minors.where_it_would_live: painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md:8 — scope the design claim to mirroring/conditioning and let flattery stand as observed behavior, not intent.

minors.title: Intercom's self-reported telemetry lands twice in-room with its vendor-self-report caveat maintainer-side only — a zombie stat under the corpus's own guard
minors.evidence: the-loop-has-a-name.md:16 '19.2% of Intercom's pull requests merge with no human reviewer... 14.6 minutes versus an org median of 75.8' and what-packaging-is.md:247 repeat the numbers as 'Intercom published the numbers'; the qualifier 'Metrics vendor-self-reported' exists only below the maintainer fence (what-packaging-is.md:272). Same pattern for skills-from-the-frontier.md:13's '267-skill plugin repo (153 contributors, 31% of R&D)'. These are the most quotable numbers in the corpus and will be re-cited as field-measured fact — the definition of a zombie stat under the corpus's own guard.
minors.where_it_would_live: One in-body clause at first use in each file ('Intercom's own published telemetry' or 'self-reported'), costing three words per site.

minors.title: 'Context engineering' — the field's handle for the training's own M1-M2 subject — never appears, so the near half's core skill is unsearchable on Tuesday
minors.evidence: Grep of every lecture body for 'context engineering': zero hits. The corpus teaches the concept thoroughly under house names ('painting the picture', 'Context is King', the Context phase, 'you prime, the LLM scales') and does deliver other field vocabulary (context rot, eval, backpressure, compound engineering). But the-loop-half-filled.md's own doctrine — 'A name is a handle... transferable to a teammate, and checkable when it stops working' — is violated for the single most-used practitioner term of 2025-26 (Karpathy's coinage, Anthropic's 'effective context engineering' guidance). A graduate cannot connect what they learned in M1-M2 to the literature written about it.
minors.where_it_would_live: One clause in curriculum/lectures/the-loop-half-filled.md's Context-law bullet (the naming lecture is exactly where a field handle belongs).

minors.title: Amplification still ships as the M1 koan 'The smart ones stay smart' — the Family-A-contested person-level leg taught un-split, un-hedged
minors.evidence: painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md:14 (body): 'If you want a shotgun, you have a shotgun. If you want a cannon, you have that too. The smart ones stay smart.' — with the maintainer watch-for '"If you want a cannon" is a koan; let it sit.' The audit's convergent (2-lens) Family-A verdict: 'Slogan neater than evidence; task-level studies point opposite for the "weak fail faster" leg (Brynjolfsson et al. found least-experienced gained most). Demote to hypothesis or split legs before teaching.' No split or hedge has been applied; the surrounding stance-mirroring mechanism covers session-level amplification but the koan asserts the contested person-level distributional claim.
minors.where_it_would_live: Same slide in painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md — keep the session-level mechanism (stance mirrored, stance is the ceiling), drop or hedge the person-level 'smart ones stay smart' leg.

minors.title: The wall (org-level: individual gains don't aggregate) remains a phenomenon without its J-curve / complementary-investment mechanism
minors.evidence: Grep 'J-curve|complementary|productivity paradox|dynamo' across corpus: zero hits. The wall appears only in crossing-language ('whether any of it crosses the wall from you to the team', the-far-half.md:18; 'a move counts when it crosses the wall', the-whole-map.md:92) — that is the cross-the-wall law, a separate finding. will-company-memory-emerge.md (M4 closer, the corpus's one org-layer beat) opens the company-layer question but never names why individual gains stall at the org (complementary reinvestment lag), leaving the audit's verdict standing: 'phenomenon without mechanism… add J-curve / complementary-investment and it becomes actionable instead of fatalistic.'
minors.where_it_would_live: curriculum/lectures/will-company-memory-emerge.md slide 2 — one mechanism bullet: gains aggregate only after the complementary redesign, which is why the company layer is worth the shape question.

minors.title: Variation-selection-retention: the audit's fold into the compound ladder (eval selects → memory retains) was never applied
minors.evidence: Grep 'variation|selection|retention|selects' across corpus bodies: zero hits. The compound-ladder bullet (the-loop-half-filled.md:19: 'One cycle is a fix. A rule is memory. A skill is reuse…') names what climbs but carries no selection clause — nothing says only verified wins earn the climb. the-loop-has-a-name.md names the eval primitive (slide 1) but never ties gate-selection to what gets encoded. The audit's triage: 'fold into compound-ladder mechanism (eval selects → memory retains) rather than a separate law' — the fold is absent from both candidate homes.
minors.where_it_would_live: One clause on the compound-ladder bullet in the-loop-half-filled.md, or on the-loop-has-a-name.md's eval slide: the gate decides what earns a rung.

strengths: naive-student: The Verification story is genuinely durable: the three failure modes arrive with real mechanism sentences (next-likely-word, finite window), the three-pattern maps piece-to-failure, and the drift-wedge/position-fix chart in what-packaging-is teaches the long-run error mechanic in guaranteed in-room text — a months-later reader can answer "why do long runs fail" and "how do I check what I didn't watch" from the page alone.

spine-coherence: The vocabulary equations the corpus does make are explicit and in guaranteed surfaces (eval=verifier=judge=gate at M6 "Three names, one thing"; memory vs context vs rules disambiguated at M4), and the compounding return gets a real in-room mechanism chain: M1's rules-inherit story ("a rule in ./CLAUDE.md is a thing every session inherits") → M3's conditioning law ("the agent conditions every move on what they hold") → M6's counter-evidence ("a rule in context is not a rule in the output") with the forcing-function repair.

groundedness: Two of the three named repairs landed cleanly and durably — the 0.85ⁿ arithmetic is subjunctive, labeled "an illustration, not a measurement", and paired with gates-reset-the-chain (backpressure.md:11-12), and the context-files law is conditioning-form (the-loop-half-filled.md:18) — and the attribution/freshness discipline (verbatim-verified quotes, dated anchors, hedged convergence claims) is stronger than most published practitioner writing.

generativity: The near half is genuinely governed and the doctrine is executed: name-the-uncertainty ("A run that can't answer 'what does this settle?' isn't aimed yet"), gap analysis ("What's between what I've got and what this task is asking for?"), rails ("Fence the reef, not the open water"), hooks ("Must happen goes in a hook; recommended stays in a prompt"), and the verifier-shape menu are all pre-action decision rules delivered in-room with mechanisms attached, and the near half gets a real consolidation closer (the-loop-half-filled) that even defines what a governor is.

canon-diff: The near half is taught as well as anything in the field's own literature: agent loop, plan discipline, packaging triad, and eval each land in guaranteed lectures as lived-move-then-name, with verified practitioner sourcing (Willison, Wei, Husain, Ronacher, Huntley, Klaassen, Bainbridge) and mechanism sentences under nearly every bolded claim.

coverage-matrix: Since the morning matrix the corpus genuinely closed its heaviest mechanism gap in-room — reliability-floor now lands Family-A-fixed via what-packaging-is.md's "One run, plotted" slide ("small errors compound silently until something outside the run measures position… A check is a position fix… An unchecked run arrives confident, and wrong") — and the spine discipline (containers govern, laws name-after, felt-at-M4/named-at-M5) holds consistently across all 27 files.

exercise-assumptions: The near half is genuinely strong: map container (M2) → five named laws with mechanisms plus one governor (M3 close) → three failure modes with statistical-machine mechanisms (M4/M5) → "eval" earned from a just-built artefact (M6) is a coherent spine, and the bolded-claim+mechanism slide discipline holds in nearly every file.

skeptical-senior: The name-after doctrine is genuinely executed, not just claimed: the three failure modes get a real mechanism ("statistical machine with a finite window") in a guaranteed lecture, the 0.85ⁿ arithmetic is explicitly hedged as illustration, quotes are verbatim-verified, and model-durability is argued (the-loop-has-a-name's "why the loop survives the model") rather than assumed.
